title: aglaia angustifolia
intro: Aglaia angustifolia, commonly known as the Angustifolia Aglaia or Thai Aglaia, is a plant species in the Meliaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include the ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity. The therapeutic actions of Aglaia angustifolia are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. Additionally, Aglaia angustifolia has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
benefits: Aglaia angustifolia has many benefits, such as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it a valuable resource for individuals suffering from arthritis, fibromyalgia, and other inflammatory conditions. This plant has also been found to exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress, thereby reducing the risk of chronic diseases like cancer, heart disease, and neurodegenerative disorders. Additionally, Aglaia angustifolia has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin infections, demonstrating its effectiveness in promoting overall well-being and improving the quality of life. By harnessing the medicinal properties of this plant, individuals can potentially enjoy improved physical health, increased energy levels, and a greater sense of vitality, ultimately leading to a more fulfilling and balanced life.
actions: Aglaia angustifolia has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its use in treating a range of health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and infections. Its antimicrobial properties help to reduce the risk of bacterial and fungal infections, improving wound healing and reducing the likelihood of scarring. Additionally, Aglaia angustifolia's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties may help to alleviate symptoms of chronic diseases such as arthritis and cardiovascular disease, enabling individuals to lead more active and comfortable lives. By reducing inflammation and improving overall health, Aglaia angustifolia's therapeutic actions can have a profound impact on daily life, enabling individuals to engage in activities they enjoy without the burden of chronic pain or illness.
intro_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", aglaia angustifolia benefits include exhibiting moderate antitumor activity and possessing antibacterial activities, as compounds 3-5 showed IC50 values ranging from 16.72 to 36.14 μM against six human tumor cell lines and MIC values of 25-100 μM against four strains of bacteria.
constituents: Aglaia angustifolia has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, terpenoids, flavonoids, and lignans, which possess various medicinal properties. These constituents have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, improving daily lives by providing relief from conditions like arthritis, acne, and cancer. For instance, the flavonoids present in Aglaia angustifolia have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, while the terpenoids have been found to exhibit antiviral and antifungal properties, making it a potential treatment for viral and fungal infections. Additionally, the plant's alkaloids have been found to exhibit analgesic and antispasmodic properties, providing relief from pain and muscle spasms, ultimately improving the overall well-being of individuals.
parts: Aglaia angustifolia has many medicinal parts, such as the roots, leaves, and seeds, which have been traditionally used in folk medicine. The roots of Aglaia angustifolia are rich in flavonoids like aglaiol and aglaiol-3'-methyl ether, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of the plant contain alkaloids like aglaiine and angustifoline, which have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. Additionally, the seeds of Aglaia angustifolia contain triterpenoids like aglaiagenin and aglaia-4-en-3-one, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
side_effects: Aglaia angustifolia can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching. In rare cases, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts can lead to liver damage or even kidney failure. Additionally, Aglaia angustifolia may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, which can worsen underlying health conditions. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, including Aglaia angustifolia, to avoid potential side effects and ensure safe usage.
